title: Number of intake plantrooms
question: How many intake plantrooms are on the network?
notes: Where there is more than one building it is normal for each building to have an intake plantroom where pipework enters the building. This is where metering equipment, strainers, and additional pumping requirements or even substations are located. In some instances the same building may have multiple intake rooms, each feeding risers.

title: Base temperature

notes: The base temperature is the external temperature below which central heating comes on. 16C to 18C is a typical range. Visit https://www.degreedays.net/base-temperature for further information.

title: Flow pipe sizing maximum velocity
question: What is the peak pipe velocity for flow (hot) pipes?
notes: Flow pipes are sized to a peak velocity to minimise size and heat loss, typically 2m/s. The upper limit is set by the materials used, and by noise limits as per CIPHE Building Engineering Services Design Guide.

title: Return pipe sizing minimum velocity
question: What is the minimum pipe velocity for (return) pipes?
notes: Return pipes are sized to a minimum velocity to maximise size and reduce pressure losses. The lower velocity of 0.5m/s is set in CIBSE Guide B (recent erratum).

title: Allow different flow and return pipe sizing
question: Can flow and return pipe sizes differ (alongside)?
notes: CIBSE Guide B recognises that maintaining velocity in flow pipes is more important than return pipes. To optimise efficiency this means that certain sectoins of pipe would be better sized with a smaller flow pipe than the return pipe. Answer yes if this is to be allowed in pipework selection.

title: Save design
question: Save design?
notes: This option opens up the menu for saving designs. Designs and data are saved to the public IPFS network for sharing. While this is not inherently secure, the data is provided a 47 character hash that is unique to the contents and impossible to guess. Data stored on IPFS is peer-to-peer and can be pinned by anyone making it persistent. We will typically pin design data for a period of 6 months after which it may be removed. See https://ipfs.io/ for mor information on IPFS (Interplanetary File system).
